Intraday post (20/Feb)

Weekly post:

OpEx, VIXpery and PCE week | Weekly post (17-20/Feb)

Python quant course is already available on my Substack-site, by an ex-Wall Street quant.

Consolidation played out just like the weekly and the daily post said.

Data came out stagflationary. GDP came out weaker than expected but desks process it as it is being distorted by the shutdowns.Core PCE remains sticky delaying the cuts, however it is already priced in and as expected on Sunday: SPX/yields correlation shifted to positive so “good news is good news”. Market is a videogame now.

Futures remain structurally weak, as they cannot gain above the marked pivot.Watch ES at 6865/75 range.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

OpEx is here, speed profile is net negative, whit long charm flows. Customers are selling volatility into both direction with more convexity to the downside. Upside vvol remains sold. If you think about the speed profile like a risk reversal, then its pivot is at 6748.80, thats where puts start to print in the net positioning.

Centroid is at 6802.68with margin of error of 6790, this is a high probability reversion point.

Upside pivot is at 6842.50 target is 6910.39The pivot is very hard to break, it is sticky and functionates as a higher level of a ladder. 6855/60 is the “event horizont” of the pivot’s stickiness, and 6875/80 is where any upward move can be braken and confirmed or rejected.

Downside pivot is at 6817.09target is 6717.06Downside is supplied until this point, below 6740/50 one should see the downside vol selling taking effect. But centroid is below the pivot, so market doesn’t really see a singificant chance.

As OI is very high and volume is lower, customer flow won’t really reveal itself. Most likely choppy price action with rebound potential with snapback by weekly post, while structurally the market remains weak (until it doesn gain above the pivots, ES 6900/05). I would avoid today’s session it is too messy. As weekly said: any bid would be only mechanical, but not rally.

As OI is very high and volume is lower, customer flow won’t really reveal itself. Most likely choppy price action with rebound potential with snapback by weekly post, while structurally the market remains weak (until it doesn gain above the pivots, ES 6900/05). I would avoid today’s session it is too messy. As weekly said: any bid would be only mechanical, but not rally.
